title: senecio bigelovii
intro: Senecio bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a liver and kidney tonic, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Senecio bigelovii include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or tea, and can also be dried and made into a powder for use in capsules or other formulations.
